headline: A Closer Look at the Stock Carnage
summary: Stocks tumbled broadly, with fears regarding access to the Strait of Hormuz escalating.    Major U.S. stock indexes slumped more than 2%. The Dow industrials fell 2.4%, on pace for its worst day since April's tariff selloff.

headline: Iran, Dip-Buyers & a Rare Buffett Stumble: Wall Street Shakes Off Its Worst Fears
summary: U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran rattled markets at the open, but seasoned dip-buyers pushed the major indices back to near-flat by the close. Meanwhile, Berkshire Hathaway's post-Buffett era gets off to a rocky start, and energy and defense stocks surge.

headline: Norwegian Cruise (NCLH) Loses 10.5% on Weak Earnings, Outlook
summary: We recently published 10 Firms Facing a Rough March So Far. Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. (NYSE:NCLH) was one of the worst performers on Monday. Norwegian Cruise fell by 10.53 percent on Monday to close at $22.18 apiece, as investors took path from a dismal earnings performance last year, and a weak outlook for 2026. […]
